Article Title: From MK Stalin to Vijay, key candidates in Tamil Nadu assembly polls
Article Text:
Vijay; MK Stalin; Edappadi K Palaniswami

Watch How Stalin, EPS, Vijay Are Outbidding Each Other On Freebies & 1 Party Is Opposing It | I Witness How Stalin, EPS, Vijay Are Outbidding Each Other On Freebies & 1 Party Is Opposing It | I Witness

Major candidates:

MK Stalin

and Udhayanidhi Stalin (DMK):

Vijay (TVK):

Edappadi K Palaniswami (AIADMK):

O Panneerselvam (DMK):

Tamilisai Soundararajan (BJP):

Nainar Nagendran (BJP):

NEW DELHI: Campaigning for the Tamil Nadu assembly elections has concluded ahead of Thursday’s single-phase polling across all 234 assembly constituencies.The election is largely expected to be a direct contest between the DMK -led ruling Secular Progressive Alliance and the National Democratic Alliance, of which AIADMK is the largest constituent in the state. Of the two national parties, Congress is aligned with the DMK, while the BJP is in alliance with the AIADMK, the DMK's arch-rival.Superstar Vijay’s Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) is also in the fray and is likely to attract youth votes.Over 4,000 candidates are in the fray, including some high-profile names.Counting of votes is scheduled for May 4.The chief minister and his deputy chief minister son are contesting from Kolathur and Chepauk-Thiruvallikeni, respectively. Stalin, a multiple-term MLA, has represented Kolathur since 2011, while Udhayanidhi is aiming to retain Chepauk-Thiruvallikeni for the DMK, having been the winning candidate in the 2021 polls.The actor-politician is leading TVK from the front and is contesting from two constituencies, Tiruchirapalli East and Perambur. The party, launched in February 2024, has fielded candidates in all 234 seats.The AIADMK general secretary and the NDA’s chief ministerial face is contesting from his home turf of Edappadi. The former chief minister is seeking a fourth successive term from Edappadi, which he has held since 2011.A one-time staunch loyalist of former chief minister and AIADMK stalwart J. Jayalalithaa, Panneerselvam made a shock move to the DMK ahead of the polls. The Stalin-led party has fielded the former chief minister from his stronghold Bodinayakkanur, which he won in three successive elections—2011, 2016 and 2021—for the AIADMK.A senior BJP leader and former Telangana governor, Tamilisai Soundararajan is contesting from the Mylapore constituency. A former president of the party’s Tamil Nadu unit, she also previously served as Puducherry’s lieutenant governor.The BJP’s state unit chief is contesting from Sattur, shifting from the Tirunelveli constituency, which he won in the 2021 elections.
Article Summary: Campaigning for the Tamil Nadu assembly elections has concluded ahead of Thursday’s single-phase polling. Over 4,000 candidates are in the fray, including some high-profile names. Counting of votes is scheduled for May 4. Of the two national parties, Congress is aligned with the DMK, while the BJP is in alliance with the AIADMK.

Article Title: Mamata Banerjee: 'CMs should get some priority': Mamata Banerjee claims flight held up for 30 mins; 'non-cooperation' from central agencies
Article Text:
Watch In Baharampur, Adhir Targets Mamata and Humayun Over Riot Allegations; TMC Stays Unfazed In Baharampur, Adhir Targets Mamata and Humayun Over Riot Allegations; TMC Stays Unfazed

Poll Do you agree that chief ministers should receive priority in travel arrangements? Yes No

NEW DELHI: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee claimed that her flight was held for half-an-hour because of the "non-cooperation from central agencies" which hampered her travel plans during the poll season. Adding that the CMs should get "some priority", she alleged that the BJP leaders get to leave."Today I was held up in the runway for half an hour on my way back. Yesterday, Jharkhand Chief Minister Hemant Soren's helicopter was stopped at Jhargram for 40 minutes," she said, addressing a rally in Belaghata.Banerjee claimed that she has been facing "non-cooperation from Central agencies such as the railways and aviation department in the past one month"."I think chief ministers should always get some priority. But here, some aviation officials seem to have forgotten that. They (BJP leaders) will leave, but you (Central officials) have to stay with us," PTI quoted her saying.The Bengal CM did not elaborate on the charges. This is not the first time that Banerjee has accused central agencies of biasness. She has accused EC and ED of the same at several poll rallies.
Article Summary: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee claims her flight was held for half- an-hour because of the "non-cooperation from central agencies" She said that the CMs should get "some priority", she alleged that the BJP leaders get to leave.

Article Title: Mamata Banerjee: ‘We will not grant you permission’: Mamata accuses EC of bias, claims denial of public meeting permission
Article Text:
Photo credit: ANI

Watch From Mamata Banerjee to Suvendu Adhikari: Leaders who will shape West Bengal elections 2026 From Mamata Banerjee to Suvendu Adhikari: Leaders who will shape West Bengal elections 2026

NEW DELHI: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee accused the Election Commission of alleged bias, claiming she was denied permission to hold a public meeting in Kolkata’s Collin Lane area while clearance for a meeting of Prime Minister Narendra Modi was granted within six hours.Addressing a rally in the Jorasanko Assembly constituency on Tuesday, Mamata criticised the poll body over what she described as unequal treatment of political parties during the ongoing election period.She said, "I had a meeting scheduled in the Collin Lane area. If a political party had been holding a meeting there, and I hadn't been granted permission, I wouldn't have given it a second thought. But, I was explicitly told (by the Election Commission), 'We will not grant you permission.' You denied permission for my meeting, yet you prioritise the Prime Minister's meeting within six hours."Earlier on Monday, the Trinamool Congress chief also alleged that the Bharatiya Janata Party had deployed selectively chosen "cherry-picked" officials at the airport to deliberately delay her travel plans. She claimed her flight was made to wait on the runway for around 30 minutes and added that a similar situation had occurred with Jharkhand Chief Minister Hemant Soren’s helicopter.Mamata Banerjee further alleged that her party had faced repeated hurdles in securing permissions for political meetings. She also accused a BJP leader, assigned election-related duties, of creating difficulties for people in the district."We had to struggle a lot to get permission for this meeting. They have cherry-picked officers. They deliberately stopped me so that my programmes would get delayed. They are not giving us permission to hold meetings. A person has come who thinks he is a 'big leader' of the BJP. I know the person very well. That person is given duty during the polls. They are troubling the people of this district. We will take revenge democratically," she said.This comes as campaigning ended for the first phase of the Assembly election in West Bengal on Tuesday, with political parties making a strong push across 152 seats where voters will cast their ballots on April 23.The West Bengal Assembly polls will be conducted in two phases for 294 seats, with 152 constituencies going to polls in the first phase and 142 in the second. Counting of votes is scheduled for May 4.
Article Summary: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee accused the Election Commission of alleged bias. She claimed she was denied permission to hold a public meeting in Kolkata’s Collin Lane area. While clearance for a meeting of Prime Minister Narendra Modi was granted within six hours.

Article Title: Karti Chidambaram: Cash is the king: Mob snatches 13.4 lakh seized from MP’s office
Article Text: SIVAGANGA: A mob snatched Rs 13.44 lakh in cash from an election flying squad, which had seized it moments ago from Sivaganga MP Karti Chidambaram's office during a raid on Monday.Police have arrested a suspect, identified as Palaniyappan. Acting on a tip-off that cash was stored for distribution to voters, the squad raided Karti's office premises in Karaikudi in Sivaganga district and recovered two bags and a cloth bag stuffed with currency.Things took a chaotic turn during verification near the officials' vehicle. Palaniyappan insisted officials count the cash, which totalled Rs 13.44 lakh, and the counting was videographed. Meanwhile, around 30 unidentified people gathered as officials prepared to secure the cash in their vehicle, and the mob suddenly snatched the bags and disappeared.Pre-empting a chase, an unidentified person took the key of the vehicle, leaving the election officials stranded.After being informed, Devakottai sub-collector Ayush Venkat Vats and Karaikudi assistant superintendent of police Ashish Puniya rushed to the spot. Following a complaint by an official, police registered a case and later arrested Palaniyappan. A manhunt has been launched to identify other members of the mob and recover the robbed cash.
Article Summary: Police have arrested a suspect, identified as Palaniyappan. The squad raided Karti's office premises in Karaikudi in Sivaganga district and recovered two bags and a cloth bag stuffed with currency. A manhunt has been launched to identify other members of the mob.

Article Title: Poll vault: Mamata alleges bias as EC shifts observer
Article Text: Merely 48 hours before polling in high-stakes Nandigram, EC replaced police observer Hitesh Choudhary with Akhilesh Singh, directing Choudhary to report to its Kolkata office. No reason was cited. CM Mamata Banerjee alleged the move was for working “impartially” and accused agencies of harassment. TMC complained police were ignoring its complaints while acting swiftly on BJP’s.
Article Summary: EC replaced police observer Hitesh Choudhary with Akhilesh Singh. No reason was cited. TMC complained police were ignoring its complaints while acting swiftly on BJP’s. CM Mamata Banerjee alleged the move was for working ‘impartially’

Article Title: Poll vault: Kejriwal warns Tamil Nadu against NDA
Article Text: AAP supremo Arvind Kejriwal Tuesday cautioned the people of Tamil Nadu to be “wary” of NDA, saying if they voted that alliance to office, it will undo DMK chief M K Stalin’s good work. On dynasty rule and corruption allegations against DMK, Kejriwal said that BJP should first look at its own party. “What is Amit Shah’s son doing? What is Rajnath Singh’s son doing? BJP is the most corrupt party today. PM Modi is giving away everything to his friends,” Kejriwal added.
Article Summary: Arvind Kejriwal warns people of Tamil Nadu to be ‘wary’ of NDA. Says if they voted that alliance to office, it will undo DMK chief M K Stalin’s good work. On dynasty rule and corruption allegations against DMK, Kejriwal said that BJP should first look at its own party.

Article Title: Rahul Gandhi: Rahul rallies in Kolkata, Serampore cancelled as police denies nod
Article Text:
.

Watch Nitish Kumar Was 'Compromised', BJP Controlled Him: Rahul Gandhi's Big Bihar Claim In Tamil Nadu Nitish Kumar Was 'Compromised', BJP Controlled Him: Rahul Gandhi's Big Bihar Claim In Tamil Nadu

KOLKATA: Police have denied permission for Rahul Gandhi ’s rallies in Kolkata and Serampore, scheduled on April 23, Bengal Congress secretary Roahan Mitra said Tuesday.Congress planned three rallies for Gandhi. For the Kolkata rallies, permission had been sought for Ramlila Park or Park Circus ground, and Metiabruz. A separate permission was sought for holding a rally at Serampore stadium.“Police have not given us permission for a single rally while others are allowed to hold rallies,” Mitra said, adding that the rallies planned for April 23 have been cancelled.Asked for a response, Bengal mantri Shashi Panja said, “Caretaker govt in the state cannot answer for the cancellation of his visit, EC can answer that.”
Article Summary: Congress planned three rallies for Gandhi. For the Kolkata rallies, permission had been sought for Ramlila Park or Park Circus ground, and Metiabruz. A separate permission was sought for holding a rally at Serampore stadium.

Article Title: Rakesh Singh: 2 FIRs against BJP's Kolkata Port candidate after clash with TMC workers
Article Text:
.

Watch Phase-wise Breakdown, Regional Divide and Political Shifts: How Bengal Voted? Phase-wise Breakdown, Regional Divide and Political Shifts: How Bengal Voted?

KOLKATA: Two FIRs were registered against BJP's Kolkata Port candidate, Rakesh Singh, following a skirmish with TMC workers on Monday night. A fortnight ago, Calcutta HC released him on interim bail in the Vidyasagar statue vandalism case.The unrest began on Monday when a BJP meet was allegedly disrupted by TMC. Singh organised a sit-in outside Ekbalpore police station to protest police inaction. TMC supporters arrived and the two groups clashed. Singh was reportedly assaulted, necessitating his hospitalisation.
Article Summary: Two FIRs were registered against BJP's Kolkata Port candidate, Rakesh Singh, following a skirmish with TMC workers on Monday night. Singh was reportedly assaulted, necessitating his hospitalisation. A fortnight ago, Calcutta HC released him on interim bail in the Vidyasagar statue vandalism case.

Article Title: Himanta Biswa Sarma: Guwahati high court asks Himanta to reply to hate speech PILs within 4 weeks
Article Text:
.

Watch TMC vs Himanta Biswa Sarma Erupts As Mamata Banerjee Row Sparks Election Commission Complaint TMC vs Himanta Biswa Sarma Erupts As Mamata Banerjee Row Sparks Election Commission Complaint

GUWAHATI: Gauhati HC on Tuesday directed the state govt and Himanta Biswa Sarma to respond within four weeks to PILs seeking action against the CM for alleged hate speeches against minority communities in the state.“It has been submitted on behalf of the state that the response affidavit shall be filed by the next date,” the HC said and posted the case for May 28.A bench of chief justice Ashutosh Kumar and justice Arun Dev Choudhury, hearing three PILs filed by the Assam State Council of Communist Party of India, Communist Party of India (Marxist) and Hirendranath Gohain and two others, decided to club them.Senior advocate Abhishek Manu Singhvi, appearing for CPM’s Assam unit, said Sarma made controversial remarks even after the HC formally issued a notice to him on the issue on Feb 26.
Article Summary: Gauhati HC on Tuesday directed the state govt and Himanta Biswa Sarma to respond within four weeks to PILs seeking action against the CM for alleged hate speeches against minority communities. A bench of chief justice Ashutosh Kumar and justice Arun Dev Choudhury decided to club them. CPM’s Assam unit said Sarma made controversial remarks even after the HC formally issued a notice to him on Feb 26.

Article Title: Kuldeep Sengar: Plea seeking death for Kuldeep Sengar nixed
Article Text:
.

Watch Unnao Rape Case: Delhi HC Rejects Kuldeep Sengar’s Plea To Suspend Sentence Unnao Rape Case: Delhi HC Rejects Kuldeep Sengar’s Plea To Suspend Sentence

NEW DELHI: In a relief for Kuldeep Sengar, the Delhi HC has dismissed the Unnao rape survivor’s plea seeking death penalty for the former BJP MLA in her father’s custodial death case, observing she failed to show “sufficient cause” for condoning a delay of 1,945 days (five-plus years) in filing an appeal against the sentence awarded by a trial court.A bench of Justices Navin Chawla and Ravinder Dudeja, on Monday, termed the “unexplained” delay as a case of “deliberate inaction and negligence”.Her father was arrested, allegedly at Sengar’s behest, under the Arms Act, and died in custody on April 9, 2018, owing to police brutality. On March 13, 2020, the court held Sengar and his brother guilty.
Article Summary: Kuldeep Sengar failed to show “sufficient cause’ for condoning a delay of 1,945 days (five-plus years) in filing an appeal against the sentence awarded by a trial court. A bench of Justices Navin Chawla and Ravinder Dudeja, on Monday, termed the “unexplained” delay as a case of “deliberate inaction and negligence”

Article Title: Congress MP moves privilege notice against PM over national address
Article Text:
.

Watch Women’s Reservation Amendment Fails In Lok Sabha, Delimitation Bill Put On Hold Women’s Reservation Amendment Fails In Lok Sabha, Delimitation Bill Put On Hold

NEW DELHI: Congress MP KC Venugopal on Tuesday filed a privilege notice against PM Narendra Modi , calling his national address after the defeat of a bill to amend the women’s reservation law in Lok Sabha contempt of the House. He said the speech criticising opposition members interferes with the MP’s independent discharge of parliamentary duties.He also termed the PM’s televised address on Saturday a violation of the model code of conduct. Assembly polls are scheduled in Bengal and Tamil Nadu.Submitting the notice, he urged Speaker Om Birla to refer it to the committee of privileges for a probe so that proceedings can be initiated against the Prime Minister.In his notice, Venugopal asserted it is a settled constitutional norm (Article 105) that aspersions cannot be cast on, and motives cannot be imputed to, the speech and voting by a member in Parliament, and doing so is tantamount to a deliberate breach of privilege and contempt of the House. The notice has been submitted under Rule 222 of the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in LS.He said the PM’s 29-minute speech, termed “address to the nation”, made direct reflections on the voting pattern of opposition members and attributed motives to them.
Article Summary: Congress MP KC Venugopal on Tuesday filed a privilege notice against PM Narendra Modi. He said the speech criticising opposition members interferes with the MP’s independent discharge of parliamentary duties. Assembly polls are scheduled in Bengal and Tamil Nadu.

Article Title: Rekha Gupta: 'TMC goons switched off lights during BJP roadshow': Delhi CM Rekha Gupta ahead of West Bengal polls
Article Text:
Photo credit: ANI

Watch From Mamata Banerjee to Suvendu Adhikari: Leaders who will shape West Bengal elections 2026 From Mamata Banerjee to Suvendu Adhikari: Leaders who will shape West Bengal elections 2026

NEW DELHI: Delhi chief minister Rekha Gupta on Monday hit out at “TMC’s goons” in West Bengal, accusing them of switching off streetlights during her roadshow in Kolkata and alleging an attempt to disrupt the event and create a security risk.Speaking to reporters, the Delhi CM said the fear once experienced by the public under the Trinamool Congress government had now shifted to its supporters. She alleged that the lights were turned off to disrupt her rally, but claimed the crowd responded by lighting up the area with mobile flashlights."This is a good thing that the fear and terror the public once felt because of the TMC government has now shifted to TMC’s own goons because of the people. Out of fear of our rally and this election campaign, they switched off the lights in the entire locality to stop the roadshow," she said."But the public responded—they turned on their mobile lights and gave their blessings and love. I thank them from the bottom of my heart. Now the people of Bengal are ready to extend their support. This darkness will not last. The darkness that the TMC government wants in people’s lives will end.The sun will rise, and the lotus will bloom," she added.Rekha Gupta was campaigning for the Bharatiya Janata Party ahead of the West Bengal Assembly elections, scheduled in two phases on April 23 and 29, with results set to be declared on May 4.She expressed confidence that the BJP would come to power in the state. In the 2021 Assembly elections, the TMC had won 213 seats, while the BJP secured 77.
Article Summary: Rekha Gupta was campaigning for the Bharatiya Janata Party ahead of the West Bengal Assembly elections, scheduled in two phases on April 23 and 29. She alleged that the lights were turned off to disrupt her rally, but claimed the crowd responded by lighting up the area with mobile flashlights.

Article Title: Census will list caste, says Amit Shah, rules out Muslim women's quota
Article Text:
Watch 'Never Be Forgiven': PM Modi Dares Opposition On Women Quota Bill, Priyanka Gandhi Questions Timing 'Never Be Forgiven': PM Modi Dares Opposition On Women Quota Bill, Priyanka Gandhi Questions Timing

New Delhi: Union home minister Amit Shah seized on Samajwadi Party MP Dharmendra Yadav's demand for quota for OBC and Muslim women to hammer home his party's avowed opposition to reservation based on religion.He also reiterated govt's stand that the caste of citizens will be mentioned during the ongoing census to reject SP chief Akhilesh Yadav's claim that Centre's decision to link women's quota to the 2011 Census is driven by its reluctance for the same. Opposing the introduction of the Constitution amendment bill, Dharmendra said in Lok Sabha that his party will never support women's reservation till a separate provision for quota for OBC and Muslim women is made in the law. "Dharmendra Yadav has made an unconstitutional demand. Our Constitution does not allow reservation based on religion at all. I will reiterate govt's resolve that giving reservation to Muslims on the basis of religion is unconstitutional," Shah said, amplifying his party's opposition to faith-based quotas on the ground that British acquiescence to the Muslim League's demand for a separate electorate for Muslims had encouraged calls for the creation of Pakistan.When Akhilesh stepped in to ask if Muslim women are not part of half of the country's population, Shah said he has no objection if SP gives all its tickets to Muslims.He said house listing is being done as part of Census 2027, and caste will be counted next. "Houses have no castes. If SP has its way, then even houses will have castes."
Article Summary: Union home minister Amit Shah seized on Samajwadi Party MP Dharmendra Yadav's demand for quota for OBC and Muslim women. He reiterated govt's stand that the caste of citizens will be mentioned during the ongoing census. Shah said he has no objection if SP gives all its tickets to Muslims.

Article Title: Taking steps to free Hindu monk Chinmoy Das: Bangladesh minister
Article Text:
.

Watch Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman Makes BIG Promise To Hindus As India Detains Hindu Cop Killer In Delhi Bangladesh PM Tarique Rahman Makes BIG Promise To Hindus As India Detains Hindu Cop Killer In Delhi







DHAKA: Bangladesh cultural affairs minister Nitai Roy Chowdhury on Friday assured leaders of the Hindu community that “appropriate steps” would be taken to ensure the early release of Hindu monk Chinmoy Krishna Das, who faces charges of sedition and murder.The Tarique Rahman-led govt is actively working to preserve the cultural heritage of minority communities while carrying out “necessary investigations”, Chowdhury, a prominent Hindu BNP leader and senior advocate, said without elaborating on the investigations being carried out.Chinmoy was arrested under the erstwhile Muhammad Yunus-led interim govt under seven charges in 2024, with a court on Thursday granting him bail in a land grabbing case. “I will raise the matter in the appropriate forum within the next few days and take necessary measures for his early release,” Chowdhury said at an event.
Article Summary: Chinmoy Krishna Das was arrested under the erstwhile Muhammad Yunus-led interim govt under seven charges in 2024. A court on Thursday granted him bail in a land grabbing case. “I will raise the matter in the appropriate forum within the next few days and take necessary measures for his early release,” Chowdhury said at an event.
